solutions.solution: (b) All the 4 cubes present on the corners will have four faces painted and two faces unpainted.
(c) 8 cubes from the upper and lower surfaces surrounded by 1 cm boundary will have two surfaces painted and remaining unpainted.
(d) 8 smaller cubes from cube (A) and one smaller cube from cube (B) will have none of its surface painted i.e., a total of 9 cubes will have none of its surface painted.
(b) 4 smaller cubes each from cubes (A) and (B) will have red, white and blue colours on one face each.
(d) 24 cubes from (A) and 11 cubes from (B) will not have red colours on any of their faces.
(d) 32 smaller cubes from (A) and 15 smaller cubes from (B) will have atleast one face red.
(c) 8 smaller cubes from (A) and four smaller cubes from (B) i.e., a total 12 cubes will have one face white and one face red and no face blue.
(c) 12 smaller cubes from (A) and 6 smaller cubes from (B) i.e., a total of 18 cubes are coloured blue, red or white on two faces each and not coloured on any other face.
(b) 16 smaller cubes from (A) and 6 smaller cubes from (B) i.e., a total of 22 cubes are coloured red, white or blue on one face each and have no other face coloured.
(b) 28 cubes from (A) and 15 cubes from (B) are coloured blue on atleast one of their face.
(c) 28 cubes from (A) and 18 cubes from (B) are coloured blue on atleast one of their face.
(d) (n – 2) × 12 = (4 – 2) × 12 = 24 cubes from (A) and (n – 2) × 12 = (3 – 2) × 12 = 12 cubes from (B) i.e., total 36 cubes are coloured on two faces only.
(a) Clearly, the four colours adjacent to green red, black, brown and white.

(c) There are four layers from the top to bottom. 1st layer has 1 block, 2nd layer has 5 blocks, 3rd layer has 3 rows of 3 blocks each, 4th layer has 4 blocks. ∴ Total blocks = 1 + 5 + 3 × 3 + 4 = 10 + 9 = 19 blocks

(g) Clearly, the four colours adjacent to green are- red, black, brown and white. (c) 91 cubes. Method I: You can see three faces at once. You can see one face only of 25 blocks on each of the three faces, for a total of 75 blocks. You can see two faces of 5 blocks along each of the three edges visible, for a total of 15 blocks. Finally, you can see three sides of the one corner block that is facing you. The total number of blocks you can see is = 75 + 15 + 1 = 91. Method II: If you remove all of the blocks you can see, you are left with a 5 × 5 × 5 cube that you cannot see. There are 6 × 6 × 6 = 216 blocks in all and you cannot see 5 × 5 × 5 = 125 of them. So, you can see = 216 – 125 = 91 blocks.

(b) The bottom layer require 3 blocks, so that it can be turned into 4 × 4 block. Second layer from bottom will need 10 blocks similarly, third layer from bottom and top layer require 13 blocks and 15 blocks respectively. ∴ Total blocks required = 3 + 10 + 13 + 15 = 41 blocks
